categories.name: Afterlife
categories.description: Explores ancient, biblical, and Christian views of life after death, including heaven and hell, resurrection, eternal punishment, purgatory, Jewish afterlife traditions, and journeys to the realm of the dead.

categories.name: Ancient World and Social Context
categories.description: Provides historical and social background for biblical and early Christian texts, including Roman politics and culture, Greco-Roman religion, literacy, archaeology, social class, wealth, and crucifixion and burial practices.

categories.name: Revelation and End-Times Interpretation
categories.description: Covers the book of Revelation and later end-times interpretations, including apocalyptic imagery, authorship, historical setting, symbolism, judgment, 666, the rapture, Armageddon, and modern uses of biblical prophecy.

categories.name: Authorship
categories.description: Examines who wrote biblical and early Christian texts, how anonymous writings received names, how sources were composed, and how secretaries affect authorship claims.

categories.name: Bart's Books
categories.description: Covers Bart's major books, including announcements, excerpts, interviews, reader questions, reviews, responses to critics, and posts developing arguments from those books.

categories.name: Bible Translations and Versions
categories.description: Covers major Bible versions, translation choices, the Septuagint, the King James Version, the NRSV, and the process of rendering ancient texts into modern languages.

categories.name: Blog Features and Personal Posts
categories.description: Covers recurring blog features, fundraising and charity updates, autobiographical posts, and personal reflections.

categories.name: Biblical Canon, Authority, and Interpretation
categories.description: Covers how writings became scripture, how biblical authority is defended or challenged, and how biblical texts are interpreted, debated, harmonized, or read as Christian scripture.

categories.name: Canonical Gospels and Acts
categories.description: Covers the canonical Gospels and Acts, including authorship, dating, sources, historical reliability, Synoptic relationships, distinctive literary topics, and Acts as Luke's narrative of early Christianity.

categories.name: Christian Origins and Expansion
categories.description: Covers the beginnings and growth of early Christianity, including early diversity, conversion, and explanations for Christianity's spread in the Roman world.

categories.name: Christology
categories.description: Examines early Christian views of Christ's identity, status, divinity, exaltation, adoption, incarnation, angelic status, and portrayal in key texts.

categories.name: Church Fathers
categories.description: Covers the Apostolic Fathers and later early church writers, including Papias, Polycarp, Eusebius, Athanasius, Didymus the Blind, Augustine, and writings or traditions associated with them.

categories.name: Early Church History
categories.description: Focuses on post-New-Testament church history, including bishops, councils, Constantine, early Christian writings, institutional changes, and the development of doctrine such as the Trinity.

categories.name: Early Judaism
categories.description: Covers Jewish groups, law, apocalyptic thought, practices, texts, and historical setting around the time of Jesus and early Christianity, including the Dead Sea Scrolls and Essenes.

categories.name: Epistles
categories.description: Covers New Testament epistles, including Pauline and non-Pauline letters, authorship, forgery, composition, persecution, and individual letters such as Philippians and 2 Thessalonians.

categories.name: Ethics and Moral Reasoning
categories.description: Covers ethical reasoning, moral problems in scripture, anti-Judaism, and moral issues in the teachings of Jesus and Paul.

categories.name: Faith, Doubt, and Nonbelief
categories.description: Covers belief, doubt, agnosticism, atheism, deconversion, fundamentalism, and personal or intellectual reasons people lose or question faith.

categories.name: Forgery
categories.description: Covers ancient and modern literary forgery, pseudepigraphy, disputed attributions, forged Pauline and non-Pauline writings, Petrine authorship questions, and modern forgery claims.

categories.name: Gnosticism, Orthodoxy, and Heresy
categories.description: Covers Gnosticism, Nag Hammadi writings, Marcion, competing Christian groups, and disputes over texts, doctrine, scripture, and authority in early Christianity.

categories.name: Hebrew Bible Texts and Traditions
categories.description: Covers Hebrew Bible books, sources, authorship, historical questions, manuscript transmission, translation traditions, apocrypha, divine beings, and interpretation.

categories.name: The Historical Jesus
categories.description: Covers topics in historical Jesus research, including Jesus' existence, apocalyptic message, messiahship, Son of Man sayings, Nazareth, Roman political context, and methods used to reconstruct his life.
topicOrder: Methods for Studying the Historical Jesus

categories.name: Historical and Critical Methods
categories.description: Covers historical and critical methods used to study biblical and early Christian texts, including dating, source criticism, redaction criticism, historicity, miracle claims, comparative analysis, and the relationship between history and theology.
topicOrder: Methods for Studying the Historical Jesus

categories.name: Jesus' Birth, Family, and Miracle Traditions
categories.description: Covers traditions about Jesus' birth, family, genealogy, possible marriage, brothers, and miracle stories in canonical and related Gospel traditions.

categories.name: Jesus' Death, Burial, and Resurrection
categories.description: Covers Gospel passion narratives, Jesus' trial and crucifixion, Roman crucifixion and burial practices, burial and empty tomb traditions, resurrection appearances, apologetic debates, and early interpretations of Jesus' death and resurrection.

categories.name: Jesus' Teachings, Ethics, and Social World
categories.description: Covers Jesus' teachings, ethical vision, economic world, views on wealth and poverty, and traditions involving women in his life and Gospel portrayals.

categories.name: Manuscripts and Textual Evidence
categories.description: Covers ancient manuscript evidence, manuscript discoveries and controversies, dating methods, and major textual finds such as Codex Sinaiticus, the Dead Sea Scrolls, Papyrus Egerton, and early Quran fragments.

categories.name: Memory and Oral Tradition
categories.description: Covers oral transmission, memory, eyewitness testimony, and debates over how traditions about Jesus and early Christian texts were preserved, reshaped, or invented before being written.

categories.name: Miracles and Supernatural Claims
categories.description: Covers miracle traditions, healings, exorcisms, visions, miracle workers, apologetic arguments, and historical questions about supernatural claims in biblical and related texts.

categories.name: New Testament Figures
categories.description: Covers named figures in and around the New Testament narratives, including Jesus' family, followers, opponents, and Roman authorities.

categories.name: Non-Canonical Christian Texts
categories.description: Covers early Christian writings outside the New Testament, including non-canonical gospels, infancy traditions, apocalypses, apocryphal acts, church manuals, apostolic writings, and miracle stories.

categories.name: Paul's Life, Mission, and Communities
categories.description: Explores Paul's life, conversion, missionary work, conflicts, persecution, churches, portrayal in Acts, and what Paul appears to have known about Jesus.

categories.name: Pauline Letters and Textual Questions
categories.description: Covers Paul's letters, including disputed authorship, Pauline forgeries, composite letters, textual variants, original-text problems, Philippians, and the transmission of Pauline writings.

categories.name: Pauline Thought and Practice
categories.description: Examines Paul's thought and practice, including salvation, Jesus' death and resurrection, Jewish law, apocalyptic expectation, slavery, women, ethics, and the Philippians Christ poem.

categories.name: Persecution and Martyrdom
categories.description: Covers persecution and martyrdom traditions, including Roman persecution, persecution in New Testament letters, Paul as persecutor or persecuted figure, apostles' deaths, Perpetua, Polycarp, and miraculous martyrdom traditions.

categories.name: Salvation, Atonement, and Judgment
categories.description: Covers how biblical and early Christian texts explain salvation, atonement, divine judgment, eternal punishment, free will, predestination, and distinctive Pauline or Luke-Acts views.

categories.name: Scholarship and Public Engagement
categories.description: Covers academic life, research, publishing, scholarly mentors, memorials, public debates, and efforts to bring biblical scholarship to wider audiences.

categories.name: Suffering and Evil
categories.description: Explores suffering and evil as theological, biblical, philosophical, and personal problems, including disasters, free will, Job, biblical explanations of suffering, and suffering as a challenge to faith.

categories.name: Teaching and Public Scholarship
categories.description: Covers courses, lectures, textbooks, media appearances, writing, publishing, and other ways scholarship is presented to public audiences.

categories.name: Textual Criticism
categories.description: Covers the study of manuscript differences, scribal changes, original texts, textual variants, methods, patristic evidence, and doctrinally significant changes to biblical texts.

categories.name: Wealth, Poverty, and Charity
categories.description: Covers posts on wealth, poverty, charity, social class, health care, and moral obligations toward people in need.

categories.name: Women, Gender, and Sexuality
categories.description: Covers posts on women, gender roles, sexuality, same-sex relations, reproduction, and related biblical or early Christian debates.
